description: GIS Agent benchmark test suite — 5 diverse test cases for systematic pipeline quality testing

cases.name: Kansas Poverty & Health Baseline
cases.description: Basic choropleth and summary statistics analysis of poverty and health insurance coverage across Kansas census tracts. The simplest full-pipeline test: ACS retrieval, join, rate computation, choropleth map, summary stats, validation, and report.

quality_criteria.data_quality: All 830 Kansas tracts present; join rate >= 95%; no tract with NULL poverty rate unless suppressed by Census; Fort Riley tract flagged as institutional
quality_criteria.analysis_rigor: Rates computed with denominator check; tracts with CV > 40% flagged as unreliable; poverty rate denominator is population for whom poverty status is determined (not total pop)
quality_criteria.map_quality: Choropleth uses quantile or natural breaks classification with 5 classes; colorblind-safe palette; state outline present; title includes vintage year; legend is readable at 200dpi
quality_criteria.report_quality: Report leads with key finding (highest-poverty tract or county cluster); methods section names ACS vintage; caveats mention MOE and ACS pooled estimate nature; sources cited

cases.name: Kansas Healthcare Access
cases.description: Multi-method healthcare access analysis combining point overlay (FQHC and HPSA locations), bivariate choropleth (poverty × uninsured), and hotspot analysis of uninsured rates. Tests the full spatial analysis capability including Moran's I gate before hotspot mapping.

quality_criteria.data_quality: FQHC/HPSA points clipped to Kansas; point count sanity-checked against HRSA website; tract join rate >= 95%
quality_criteria.analysis_rigor: Global Moran's I run before hotspot analysis; if not significant (p > 0.05), hotspot map replaced with 'spatially random' finding note; bivariate uses 3x3 Stevens scheme with correct variable assignment (row=poverty, col=uninsured); FDR correction applied to hotspot results
quality_criteria.map_quality: Point overlay uses distinct marker style from choropleth background; bivariate legend is full 3x3 grid with axis labels; hotspot map shows HH/HL/LH/LL/NS categories with counts in legend
quality_criteria.report_quality: Report synthesizes all three methods into a coherent access narrative; does not treat each map as independent; notes data limitations (HRSA lag, FQHC catchment not modeled)

cases.name: South Dakota Demographic Baseline
cases.description: Simple demographic baseline for South Dakota census tracts — population, race/ethnicity, income, and poverty. Tests retrieval, processing, and basic choropleth output for a smaller state (~260 tracts) with significant tribal land areas that require careful handling.

quality_criteria.data_quality: All ~265 SD tracts present; tribal land tracts identified and noted (Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Standing Rock); tracts with very high MOE flagged; no suppressed values treated as zero
quality_criteria.analysis_rigor: Income choropleth uses median (not mean); poverty rate uses correct denominator; racial composition computed as shares not raw counts; small-N tracts noted for unreliable estimates
quality_criteria.map_quality: Map extent covers all of SD without excessive whitespace; tribal area tracts visually distinguishable if possible; title clear and includes vintage
quality_criteria.report_quality: Report notes South Dakota's unique demographic context (large Native American population, tribal sovereignty areas); does not make causal claims about income disparities

cases.name: Chicago Food Access Analysis
cases.description: Food access analysis for Chicago (Cook County) combining USDA Food Access Research Atlas data with point overlay of grocery stores, and service area / drive-time analysis for food desert identification. Tests federal data retrieval (USDA) and the service area analysis capability.

quality_criteria.data_quality: USDA data joined to Cook County tracts at >= 90% rate; grocery store points clipped to county; known data issue documented (USDA URL sometimes dead — use cached or alternative source)
quality_criteria.analysis_rigor: Food desert definition clearly stated (USDA definition vs custom distance-based); if service areas used, method described (OSMnx isochrone or Euclidean buffer); correlation with poverty/income computed
quality_criteria.map_quality: Food desert tracts clearly highlighted; grocery store points visible at city scale; legend distinguishes food desert vs low-access vs adequate access categories
quality_criteria.report_quality: Report contextualizes Chicago's specific geography (lake on east, dense city core vs suburbs); avoids deficit framing of affected communities; notes USDA definition controversy

known_issues: USDA Food Access Atlas download URL changes frequently — verify before running
OSMnx service area analysis may time out on Pi hardware — fall back to buffer analysis

cases.name: LA County Environmental Justice
cases.description: Multi-indicator environmental justice analysis for Los Angeles County combining EPA EJScreen data (PM2.5, proximity to hazardous waste, cancer risk) with demographic vulnerability indicators (poverty, race/ethnicity, linguistic isolation). Tests multi-source data integration and index construction.
data_sources: EPA EJScreen (block group → aggregated to tract)

quality_criteria.data_quality: EJScreen aggregation from block group to tract documented with methodology (area-weighted or population-weighted); all 2346 LA County tracts present; EJScreen percentile interpretation noted (national vs state percentile)
quality_criteria.analysis_rigor: Environmental and vulnerability indices constructed with documented component weights; bivariate choropleth identifies double-burden tracts (high env risk + high vulnerability); hotspot analysis optional but recommended for spatial clustering
quality_criteria.map_quality: County-level maps legible despite 2346-tract density; color schemes distinguish environmental from demographic layers; bivariate uses appropriate diverging or 3x3 scheme
quality_criteria.report_quality: Report identifies specific high-burden communities by name where possible; cites CalEnviroScreen as context; notes EJScreen methodology limitations; avoids treating model output as ground truth

known_issues: EJScreen block group → tract aggregation requires care; use population-weighted mean for rate indicators
Large tract count (2346) may slow choropleth rendering — use DPI=150 if needed
